title: Neoporteria reichei
taxonomy: Neoporteria reichei belongs to the family Cactaceae. It is a genus of cacti within the subfamily Neopetaloideae. The species is native to Chile and is classified as a type of tree-like cactus. Neoporteria reichei is characterized by its solitary stem, green stems, and white flowers. It is also known for its unique, tree-like growth habit and spiny stems.

intro: Neoporteria reichei, also known as Reiche's prickly pear cactus, is a species of cactus in the genus Neoporteria. This plant has been found to offer various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting weight loss due to its high fiber content. The bioactive constituents of Neoporteria reichei include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Neoporteria reichei is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and jams, as well as being used in the production of skincare products and food supplements.
